title: meyera umbellata
intro: Meyera umbellata, also known as the "Umbellata tree" or "Gutta gum tree", is a tropical tree species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India, China, and Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of the Meyera umbellata plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Meyera umbellata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Meyera umbellata include the bark being used to make a decoction, and the roots being used to make a powder or tincture.
